title: oxalis erythrorhiza
intro: Oxalis erythrorhiza, also known as the Purple Wood Sorrel, is a type of flowering plant belonging to the Oxalidaceae family. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include its ability to inhibit the growth of certain bacteria and fungi, and its potential anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Oxalis erythrorhiza include its use in treating digestive issues, such as diarrhea and stomach ulcers, as well as its potential anti-cancer properties. Oxalis erythrorhiza is typically prepared as a tea, tincture, or infusion, and can be consumed fresh as a salad green, although its oxalic acid content should be considered when using it in food preparations.
